name: Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular
name_original: Pantano y Parque de la Grajera, Valle del Ebro – circular
region_intro: Jogging around Parque de La Grajera offers access to an extensive natural space characterized by its large reservoir, green areas, and woodlands. The park, covering 87 hectares, features a 32-hectare reservoir at its center, providing a scenic backdrop for running. A network of paths, including sections of the Camino de Santiago, traverses varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. This protected natural space serves as a green lung for the city, offering a diverse environment for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Parque de La Grajera The most popular jogging route is Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes varied terrain around the reservoir and through parkland. Another top favourite among local runners is La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Restaurante El 19, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This route offers a loop around the central reservoir, providing consistent views of the water and surrounding green spaces. Local runners also love the La Grajera Reservoir loop from La Grajera (M3-6), a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through the park's green spaces and alongside the reservoir, often completed in about 31 minutes. Jogging around Parque de La Grajera is defined by its large reservoir, extensive green spaces, and sections of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging paths with elevation changes. The routes in Parque de La Grajera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Parque de La Grajera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find in Parque de La Grajera?
A: Parque de La Grajera offers a diverse range of running routes suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, including scenic loops around the central reservoir, as well as more challenging paths with significant elevation changes. The park features over 200 routes, with a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Parque de La Grajera?
A: Yes, Parque de La Grajera has plenty of easy and beginner-friendly options. Many paths around the reservoir are well-maintained and relatively flat, perfect for a relaxed run. There are approximately 30 easy routes available, making it a great place to start or enjoy a gentle jog.

Q: Can I find challenging running routes with elevation changes in the park?
A: Absolutely. For runners seeking a more intense workout, Parque de La Grajera offers routes with significant elevation changes. These can be up to 10 miles long and require good fitness. An example of a more challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38182799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente antigua N-120 loop from Aparcamiento para camiones&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated difficult and features substantial climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Parque de La Grajera?
A: The park's centerpiece is the large &lt;strong&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, which provides a beautiful backdrop for many runs and is an important lagoon ecosystem. You'll also pass through extensive green spaces and woodlands. Keep an eye out for diverse avian species, as the reservoir is excellent for birdwatching. A significant part of the famous &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago&lt;/strong&gt; pilgrimage route also traverses the park, leading to the 'Alto de La Grajera' with panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, circular routes around the reservoir are very popular. These loops offer consistent views of the water and surrounding nature. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Restaurante El 19&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.1 km path that provides a complete circuit of the central reservoir.

Q: Is Parque de La Grajera suitable for family jogging or walks?
A: Yes, the park is very family-friendly. Its well-maintained, mostly paved paths around the reservoir are suitable for all ages and fitness levels, making it ideal for family jogs or leisurely walks. The park also includes amenities like picnic areas and a children's play area, adding to its appeal for families.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Parque de La Grajera?
A: Parque de La Grajera is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy your run with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the park's natural beauty and respect other visitors.

Q: What amenities are available for runners in Parque de La Grajera?
A: Runners can benefit from various amenities within the park. There are marked paths for easy navigation, observation points, and picnic areas. You'll also find a café-restaurant with a terrace overlooking the reservoir, perfect for a post-run refreshment. The park also features an 18-hole golf course and other sports facilities.

Q: How do other runners rate the trails in Parque de La Grajera?
A: The running routes in Parque de La Grajera are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, the scenic reservoir views, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Can I combine my run with a section of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Absolutely! A significant portion of the historic Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route passes directly through Parque de La Grajera. You can follow the distinct yellow arrows, which will lead you to the 'Alto de La Grajera,' offering stunning panoramic views of the entire park and the city of Logroño. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago – La Grajera Reservoir loop from La Grajera (M3-6)&lt;/a&gt; for a run that incorporates this iconic path.

Q: What is the typical distance of a running route around the reservoir?
A: Many popular running routes around the reservoir are moderate loops, typically ranging from 5 to 7 kilometers (3 to 4.5 miles). For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.6 km (4.1 miles) route that offers varied terrain around the reservoir and through parkland.
tips: Official view point on the lake.
